title: undefined and null Check
description: Missing values, strict checks, and labeling behavior.

questions.explanation: Unassigned `let` is `undefined`. `null` is not `undefined`, but `b === null` is `true`.
An empty string is neither `undefined` nor `null` — it is a real value.
`typeof null` is `"object"` for historical reasons. Use `value === null` to test null.

questions.prompt: When should you use `null` instead of `undefined`?

options.text: When your code intentionally means "known to be absent"
Whenever a variable is declared with `let`
Never — they are identical in all cases

questions.explanation: `undefined` is the default "no value yet"; `null` is an explicit empty marker.
Use strict `===` checks. `null` matches the second branch, not the `undefined` branch.
